theme: What's Your Favourite Flavour of Crisp?

sentences.english: In Britain we call them crisps and you might know them as potato chips, but whatever you call them, these fried potato slices are probably the world's favourite snack.
sentences.korean: 영국에서는 크리스프, 여러분은 포테이토 칩이라고 알 수도 있는데, 뭐라 부르든 이 튀긴 감자 조각은 아마 세계에서 가장 인기 있는 간식이에요.

sentences.english: Crisps might be popular but with lots of oil and salt they're not the healthiest food.
sentences.korean: 감자칩은 인기 있지만 기름과 소금이 많아 가장 건강한 음식은 아니에요.

sentences.english: So, why do we love them so much?
When you bite into a crisp, you get that auditory crunch, and it's so satisfying.
sentences.korean: 감자칩을 베어 물면 청각적 바삭함이 매우 만족스러워요.

sentences.english: People hear that and they want more.
A snowball effect happens, one chip becomes two, two becomes ten, and before you know it, the bag's gone.
sentences.korean: 눈덩이 효과로 한 개가 두 개, 두 개가 열 개가 되고 어느새 봉지가 비었어요.

sentences.english: Because you could do so many things — dip it in almost everything, adding some guacamole, spicy salsa or just sour cream.
sentences.korean: 거의 모든 것에 찍을 수 있고, 과카몰리나 매운 살사, 사워크림을 곁들일 수 있으니까요.

quiz.question: What does '개인적 취향, 개인적 선호' mean in English?
